Per FIRST-PRINCIPLES universal-substrate posture, the Visit > Activity > Task hierarchy handles ANY assessment without modeling specifics — a DICOM imaging Activity and a blood-draw Activity are the same TOP shape. The OOUX Visit->Site direct edge is corrected: Visit.forStudySite -> StudySite (1..1).
top_levels.ooux_editor_note: v0.5.0 introduction (2026-05-09): sixth TOP top-level. Visit-occurrences are runtime-created by EDC/scheduling/eVisit systems, NOT USDM-ingest-derivable (template-side is VisitDefinition; runtime-side is Visit). Per Visit planning Decision 4 (revised), Activity + Task are universal sub-objects under Visit; specialization is content (taskValue polymorphism + biomedicalConceptCode), never entity shape. visitStatus is a NGSI-LD temporal property for trajectory queryability (parallel to Participant.participantStatus). visitMode 6-state enum per ICH E6(R3) Annex 2 DCT framing. Three Visit sub-objects: Activity (universal work unit), Task (universal leaf data-capture, with polymorphic taskValue), VisitObservation (operator-narrative escalation-to-Event sub-object per OOUX Path C). FHIR Questionnaire projection is the canonical EHR-rendering target: VisitDefinition + Activity-templates + Task-templates project to FHIR Questionnaire JSON (consumable by Epic, Cerner, SMART on FHIR apps); Visit + Activities + Tasks project to FHIR QuestionnaireResponse.

attributes.name: visitId
attributes.doc: Globally unique NGSI-LD identifier.

attributes.name: visitNumber
attributes.doc: Numeric ordering per protocol (matches VisitDefinition.visitNumber when definedBy is populated). Maps to SDTM VISITNUM.

attributes.name: visitName
attributes.doc: Operator-friendly name. For UNSCHEDULED visits, free text describing the reason. Maps to SDTM VISIT.

attributes.name: visitMode
attributes.doc: Visit modality per ICH E6(R3) Annex 2 DCT framing. IN_PERSON_CLINIC for traditional clinic visits; IN_PERSON_HOME for home health; REMOTE_VIDEO for telehealth; REMOTE_PHONE for phone visits; REMOTE_ASYNC for ePRO/eDiary capture without live interaction; HYBRID for mixed-modality visits. FHIR Encounter.virtualService + Encounter.location are the projection targets.

attributes.name: visitStatus
attributes.doc: Visit lifecycle state. NGSI-LD temporal property (validFrom/validUntil per value) for trajectory queryability. SCHEDULED for booked-but-not-started; IN_PROGRESS during active visit; COMPLETED for per-protocol completion; PARTIALLY_COMPLETED when some Activities not performed (rescheduled separately); MISSED for didn't-happen-no-makeup; OUT_OF_WINDOW when actual datetime falls outside visitDefinition.visitWindow; UNSCHEDULED for ad-hoc (urgent care, AE workup, sponsor-requested). FHIR Encounter.status is the projection target.

attributes.name: plannedStartDate
attributes.doc: Planned visit date per SOA / scheduling system. What the operator/coordinator booked.

attributes.name: plannedEndDate
attributes.doc: Planned end date (typically same day; multi-day visits possible for inpatient procedures or PK time-courses).

attributes.name: actualStartDate
attributes.doc: Actual visit start date. Empty for SCHEDULED visits not yet started. Maps to SDTM SVSTDTC.

attributes.name: actualStartTime
attributes.doc: Actual start time-of-day where captured. Operational completeness.

attributes.name: actualEndDate
attributes.doc: Actual visit end date. Maps to SDTM SVENDTC.

attributes.name: actualEndTime
attributes.doc: Actual end time-of-day where captured.

attributes.name: visitDay
attributes.doc: Days from study anchor (typically randomization or first dose). Computed from actualStartDate when definedBy is populated. Maps to SDTM VISITDY.

attributes.name: protocolDeviationCode
attributes.doc: Sponsor-defined deviation code when visitStatus=OUT_OF_WINDOW or other deviation. Free text or coded; aligns with sponsor deviation taxonomy.

attributes.name: unscheduledReason
attributes.doc: Reason for an unscheduled visit (urgent care, AE workup, sponsor-requested follow-up). Required when visitStatus=UNSCHEDULED. Maps to SDTM SVUPDES.

attributes.name: tags
attributes.doc: Comma-separated user-defined tags.

attributes.name: validFrom
attributes.doc: NGSI-LD temporal property: when this Visit record became operationally valid.

attributes.name: validUntil
attributes.doc: NGSI-LD temporal property: when this Visit record was superseded or closed.

relationships.name: forParticipant
relationships.doc: The Participant whose visit this is. PROV: this Visit Activity is wasAttributedTo this Participant. Maps to FHIR Encounter.subject (via ResearchSubject.subject linkage).

relationships.name: forStudySite
relationships.doc: The StudySite hosting this visit (operational owner regardless of physical location — telehealth visits still have a StudySite of record). OOUX Visit->Site corrected to Visit->StudySite per the operational hierarchy. Maps to FHIR Encounter.serviceProvider + Encounter.location.

relationships.name: definedBy
relationships.doc: The VisitDefinition (protocol template) this Visit instantiates. 0..1 because UNSCHEDULED visits don't have a corresponding template. PROV: wasInformedBy semantics — the template informed the actual visit's expected scope.

relationships.name: hasActivity
relationships.doc: Activities performed at this Visit (universal sub-objects: vitals, MRI, ePRO, biopsy, IP-admin, etc.). Per FIRST-PRINCIPLES universal-substrate posture, Activity is the same TOP shape regardless of activity type.

relationships.name: hasTask
relationships.doc: Tasks (leaf data-capture units) performed at this Visit. Each Task has belongsToActivity referencing its parent Activity within the same Visit. Polymorphic taskValue handles all data shapes (numeric, text, coded, URI references, structured).

relationships.name: hasVisitObservation
relationships.doc: Operator-narrative observations captured during the visit that don't fit a structured Activity slot (per OOUX Path C reportability handoff). May escalate to Event when relevant.

relationships.name: partOf
relationships.doc: Parent Visit this Visit is part of (multi-part visits, follow-up linkage). Maps to FHIR Encounter.partOf and OMOP preceding_visit_occurrence_id (linked-list).

relationships.name: hasAdverseEvent
relationships.doc: Adverse events identified at or attributed to this Visit. Flagged-missing until Event lifts.
relationships._targetMissing: Event is a top-level in OOUX-locked-9 but not yet in TOP source; lifts in v0.7+.

relationships.name: hasTag
relationships.doc: User-defined tags.
relationships._targetMissing: Tag flagged-missing as before.

ctas.note: Create a new Visit record with visitStatus=SCHEDULED for an upcoming participant visit. Populates plannedStartDate, definedBy, forParticipant, forStudySite.
Transition visitStatus from SCHEDULED to IN_PROGRESS. Captures actualStartDate/Time.
Transition to COMPLETED after all expected Activities performed; captures actualEndDate/Time.
Move a SCHEDULED visit to a new date; original visit transitions to RESCHEDULED, new SCHEDULED Visit created with partOf reference.
Cancel a SCHEDULED visit (early termination, withdrawal, etc.); transitions to CANCELLED.
Mark a Visit as MISSED when it didn't happen and no make-up is currently scheduled.
When a Visit's actual datetime falls outside the visitDefinition.visitWindow, transition to OUT_OF_WINDOW and populate protocolDeviationCode.
Create a Visit not in the SOA (urgent care, AE workup, sponsor-requested); visitStatus=UNSCHEDULED; populates unscheduledReason.
Add an Activity sub-object to the Visit (Vitals, Blood Draw, MRI, ePRO session, IP administration, etc.).
Add a Task sub-object capturing a single data point (BP, MRI Study reference, PHQ-9 item, drug dose). Task references its parent Activity via belongsToActivity.
Add a VisitObservation sub-object for operator-narrative items that don't fit structured Activities.
Flag a VisitObservation as escalated; populates escalatedTo when Event lifts.

sub_objects.role: the universal work unit performed at a Visit
sub_objects.description: Activity is the universal work unit at a Visit — Vitals, Blood Draw, MRI, ePRO session, IP administration, ECG, biopsy, sequencing draw, ADAS-Cog session, anything. Per FIRST-PRINCIPLES universal-substrate posture, the SAME entity shape handles all activity types; specialization is content (biomedicalConceptCode + activityType for filter/sort + linked Equipment + governing Document), never entity shape. activityStatus is a NGSI-LD temporal property. PROV: each Activity is a prov:Activity with wasAssociatedWith Person agents, used Equipment, governed-by Documents.

crosswalks.fhir: fhir:Procedure (procedural Activities); fhir:Observation (assessment Activities); resource selection by activityType
crosswalks.sdtm: varies by activityType: VS / EX / PR / LB / EG / IE / CM (each domain row corresponds to Activity + its Tasks)
crosswalks.usdm: usdm:Activity (template-side; runtime-side has no USDM analog)

attributes.name: activityName
attributes.doc: Operator-friendly name (e.g., 'Vital Signs', 'MRI Brain', 'ECOG Performance Status', 'Drug X Administration', 'EQ-5D-5L').

attributes.name: activityType
attributes.doc: Coarse categorization for operator sort/filter, NOT for specialization. Per FIRST-PRINCIPLES universal-substrate posture, the entity shape is the same for all types — activityType is a hint, not a discriminator that changes structure.

attributes.name: activityStatus
attributes.doc: Lifecycle of this Activity. NGSI-LD temporal property.

attributes.name: notPerformedReason
attributes.doc: When activityStatus=NOT_PERFORMED, the reason (subject refused, equipment failure, scheduling conflict, etc.). Required by SHACL when status indicates non-performance.

attributes.name: biomedicalConceptCode
attributes.doc: NCIt C-code (or LOINC where applicable) identifying what the Activity IS. References COSMoS BC catalog. The discriminator that operators (and downstream systems) use to know 'this is a Vital Signs activity' vs 'this is an MRI Brain activity' — content, not shape.

attributes.name: validFrom
attributes.doc: NGSI-LD temporal property: operational start time of the Activity within the Visit.

attributes.name: validUntil
attributes.doc: NGSI-LD temporal property: operational end time.

relationships.name: performedBy
relationships.doc: Person(s) who performed the Activity. PROV: prov:wasAssociatedWith. Drawn from forStudySite.delegatesAuthorityTo for delegated tasks.

relationships.name: usedEquipment
relationships.doc: Equipment used to perform the Activity (centrifuge, MRI scanner, infusion pump, ECG machine, etc.). PROV: prov:used. The Equipment's calibration/credential chain is queryable from here.

relationships.name: governedBy
relationships.doc: Document(s) that govern how this Activity should be performed (protocol section, SOP, lab manual). Document carries the section anchor. Per the chain-of-custody mock-up: 'Per shipping SOP-2026-CL-019' is a governedBy reference.

sub_objects.description: Task is the universal leaf data-capture unit at a Visit (one Task per measurement / response / dose / observation). Per FIRST-PRINCIPLES universal-substrate posture, Task handles ANY captured data via polymorphic taskValue (NUMERIC / TEXT / CODED / URI_REFERENCE / STRUCTURED / DATE / IMAGE_REFERENCE). A BP measurement, an MRI Study Instance UID reference, a PHQ-9 item response, a drug dose record, a tumor lesion measurement — all the same TOP entity shape. The specialization lives in content. Each Task carries observedAt as Property metadata on taskValue (NOT a separate flat performedDateTime attribute) per NGSI-LD temporal conventions. Maps to FHIR Observation (one Observation per Task) and per-domain SDTM rows (VS/LB/EX/etc.).

crosswalks.fhir: fhir:Observation (one per Task); fhir:QuestionnaireResponse.item.answer (when in a Questionnaire context)
crosswalks.sdtm: varies by domain: VS / LB / EX / EG / IE / etc. — each Task projects to a domain row keyed by USUBJID + VISITNUM + TEST

attributes.name: taskName
attributes.doc: Operator-friendly name (e.g., 'Systolic BP', 'MRI Study Instance UID', 'PHQ-9 Item 1', 'Drug X dose').

attributes.name: taskValueType
attributes.doc: Discriminates the shape of taskValue. NUMERIC for numeric measurements; TEXT for free text; CODED for enum/concept-code values; URI_REFERENCE for URIs to external artifacts (DICOM PACS, S3, lab LIS); STRUCTURED for nested object values (lesion measurements with components, BP with sys/dia/MAP); DATE for captured dates; IMAGE_REFERENCE for radiology/pathology image URIs.

attributes.name: taskValue
attributes.doc: The captured value, polymorphic per taskValueType. NUMERIC values held as string for SHACL flexibility; deployment-side typing applies at projection time. NGSI-LD observedAt Property metadata captures when the value was measured/observed (NOT a separate performedDateTime attribute).

attributes.name: taskUnit
attributes.doc: UCUM unit where applicable (e.g., '[mmHg]', 'cm', 'mg', 'beats/min'). Empty for taskValueType=TEXT/CODED/URI_REFERENCE/etc. where unit doesn't apply.

attributes.name: biomedicalConceptCode
attributes.doc: NCIt or LOINC C-code identifying what the Task IS (the BC catalog lookup). e.g., NCIt:C25298 for Systolic BP, LOINC:44261-6 for PHQ-9 Q1, NCIt:C16809 for MRI Brain.

attributes.name: taskStatus
attributes.doc: Lifecycle of the Task. NGSI-LD temporal property.

attributes.name: notPerformedReason
attributes.doc: When taskStatus=NOT_PERFORMED. Required by SHACL.

attributes.name: observedAt
attributes.doc: When the value was observed/measured. Canonical NGSI-LD Property metadata for measurement timestamps. Maps to FHIR Observation.effectiveDateTime and SDTM per-domain DTC fields.

relationships.name: belongsToActivity
relationships.doc: The parent Activity within the same Visit. Tasks group under Activities for operator sort/filter and projection-adapter routing (e.g., all VS-domain Tasks in one Vitals Activity).

relationships.name: performedBy
relationships.doc: The Person who captured this Task. PROV: prov:wasAssociatedWith.

sub_objects.role: operator-narrative observation captured during a visit
sub_objects.description: VisitObservation captures something the operator notices during a visit that doesn't fit a structured Activity / Task slot — observational notes, unexpected events, patient-reported context. Per OOUX Path C, may escalate to a categorized Event (when Event lifts) via escalatedTo. Distinct from Task (which is structured data capture). PROV: each VisitObservation is a prov:Activity that wasInformedBy by the parent Visit; if escalated, the resulting Event was wasInformedBy this VisitObservation.

crosswalks.sdtm: varies: NV (Non-Standard Variables), DV (Protocol Deviations), or domain-specific note fields

attributes.name: observationText
attributes.doc: The operator's narrative observation.

attributes.name: observationCategory
attributes.doc: Coarse categorization. SAFETY_SIGNAL escalates to AE workflow; PROTOCOL_DEVIATION goes to deviation tracking; DATA_DISCREPANCY into query workflow; OPERATIONAL_NOTE for routine notes; OTHER for catch-all.

attributes.name: observationDate
attributes.doc: When the observation was recorded.

attributes.name: observationTime
attributes.doc: Time-of-day the observation was recorded.

attributes.name: escalated
attributes.doc: True if this observation has been escalated to a categorized Event. SHACL soft warning: escalated=true should have escalatedTo populated.

attributes.name: escalationDate
attributes.doc: Date the escalation occurred.

relationships.name: recordedBy
relationships.doc: The Person who recorded the observation (typically a CRA, coordinator, or PI). PROV: prov:wasAssociatedWith.

relationships.name: escalatedTo
relationships.doc: When escalated, the categorized Event that resulted from this observation. PROV: the Event was wasInformedBy this observation.
relationships._targetMissing: Event is a top-level in OOUX-locked-9 but not yet lifted; flagged-missing until v0.7+.

top_levels.role: regulatory / ethics / safety committee with review authority over a Study or StudySite
top_levels.description: An OversightBody is a committee that holds review authority over a Study or StudySite — IRB (Institutional Review Board) for site-level ethics review, EC (Ethics Committee) for the EU equivalent, DSMB / IDMC (Data Safety Monitoring Board / Independent Data Monitoring Committee) for sponsor-level interim safety review, Steering Committee for cross-site governance, CEC (Clinical Events Committee) for endpoint adjudication. Per FIRST-PRINCIPLES universal-substrate posture, OversightBody is a substrate primitive (every regulated trial has oversight bodies regardless of therapeutic area). The oversight body holds authority; the review activities happen through ReviewDecision sub-objects.
top_levels.ooux_editor_note: v0.5.1 introduction (2026-05-09): seventh TOP top-level. Per OOUX-locked-9 sequencing. Un-flag-misses three existing relationships: Sponsor.interfacesWith, StudySite.hasIRB, Study.hasOversightBody. Per the temporal+PROV native commitment, OversightBody is prov:Agent (it bears responsibility); ReviewDecision sub-object is prov:Activity (the review event). reviewType enum carries the operator-grounded discrimination (IRB / EC / DSMB / IDMC / STEERING_COMMITTEE / CEC / OTHER) without splitting into specialized entities — universal substrate posture preserved. The cross-jurisdictional reality is that the same human committee can play different oversightBodyType roles on different studies (a hospital IRB can act as central IRB for some studies and local IRB for others); each role is a separate OversightBody record per the per-Org-per-Role pattern from Sponsor.

crosswalks.ich: ICH E6(R3) Section 5 (IRB/EC); ICH E9 (DSMB/IDMC); ICH E2A (Safety Reporting)

attributes.name: oversightBodyId
attributes.doc: Globally unique NGSI-LD identifier.

attributes.name: oversightBodyName
attributes.doc: Display name (e.g., 'Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center IRB', 'ONCO-423 Independent Data Monitoring Committee').

attributes.name: oversightBodyType
attributes.doc: Type of oversight body. IRB for US institutional review boards; EC for European ethics committees; DSMB / IDMC are interchangeable terms for sponsor-level interim safety review boards; STEERING_COMMITTEE for cross-site governance; CEC for clinical events committees doing endpoint adjudication; OTHER for catch-all.

attributes.name: scope
attributes.doc: Scope of authority. LOCAL for site-specific (typical IRB at a single institution); CENTRAL for multi-site coverage (a single IRB serving multiple sites); SPONSOR for sponsor-level (DSMB/IDMC); REGULATORY for regulator-affiliated; OTHER for catch-all.

attributes.name: registrationNumber
attributes.doc: Registration identifier (e.g., FDA OHRP IRB registration number IRB00000123, FWA00001234, EU EC reference).

attributes.name: registrationAuthority
attributes.doc: The authority that issued the registration (e.g., 'FDA OHRP', 'EU CTIS', 'NHS HRA').

attributes.name: registrationCountry
attributes.doc: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the body is registered.

attributes.name: address
attributes.doc: Mailing address (line1, city, region, postalCode, country).

attributes.name: operatingProcedure
attributes.doc: URI to the body's published operating procedure / charter / SOP. References a Document horizontal entity for full lineage when uploaded.

attributes.name: establishedDate
attributes.doc: Date the body was established.

attributes.name: status
attributes.doc: Lifecycle state. NGSI-LD temporal property — bodies can be suspended (regulatory hold) or disbanded after study completion.

attributes.name: validFrom
attributes.doc: NGSI-LD temporal property: when this OversightBody record became operationally valid for the trial.

attributes.name: validUntil
attributes.doc: NGSI-LD temporal property: when this OversightBody record was superseded or closed.

relationships.name: belongsToOrganization
relationships.doc: The Organization that hosts / sponsors / convened the oversight body. 0..1 because some bodies (especially DSMBs) are convened ad-hoc without a hosting Organization. PROV: the Organization is a higher-order Agent.

relationships.name: hasMember
relationships.doc: Committee members. Each Person has expertise relevant to the body's mandate (clinical, statistical, ethical, patient advocacy). PROV: each member is a prov:Agent acting on behalf of the OversightBody.

relationships.name: chair
relationships.doc: The committee chair. 0..1 because some bodies operate with co-chairs or rotating chairs.

relationships.name: reviewsStudy
relationships.doc: Studies this body has review authority over. 0..N: an IRB reviews many studies; a DSMB typically reviews one.

relationships.name: reviewsStudySite
relationships.doc: StudySites this body has review authority over. Site-level IRBs typically review per-StudySite; central IRBs review multiple StudySites under one Study.
relationships._targetMissing: StudySite is a horizontal but the relationship target resolves correctly; no flag needed in v0.5.1.

ctas.note: Create a new OversightBody record with registration number, scope, and member list.
Add or remove Person members; track validFrom/validUntil for membership terms.
Create a ReviewDecision record (sub-object when lifted) with decision, decision date, conditions, next review date.
Transition status to SUSPENDED (regulatory hold or institutional suspension).
Transition status to DISBANDED after study close-out and document retention period ends.

top_levels.role: the drug, biologic, device, or combination product under investigation
top_levels.description: An InvestigationalProduct (IP) is the substance, biologic, medical device, or combination being studied in a Study. Per FIRST-PRINCIPLES universal-substrate posture, IP is a single entity covering all product types — drugs, biologics, devices, diagnostics, combination products — discriminated by investigationalProductType enum. Specialization is content (productCode, dosageForm, mechanismOfAction, regulatorySource); not entity shape. Per ICH E6(R3) Section 2.7 and 21 CFR 312.6, IPs require lot tracking, blinded kit packaging (when applicable), and dispensation accountability. TOP models these via two sub-objects: Lot (per-batch manufacturing identity) and Kit (per-blinded-packaging-unit). Dispensation events live as Visit Activities (activityType=IP_ADMINISTRATION) per the universal Visit > Activity > Task substrate; no separate Dispensation entity needed.
top_levels.ooux_editor_note: v0.6.0 introduction (2026-05-09): eighth TOP top-level. Per OOUX-locked-9 sequencing. Un-flag-misses Sponsor.supplies + Study.suppliesInvestigationalProduct. Per FIRST-PRINCIPLES universal-substrate posture, single IP entity with investigationalProductType enum discriminator (DRUG / BIOLOGIC / DEVICE / DIAGNOSTIC / COMBINATION_PRODUCT / OTHER). The Lot sub-object captures per-batch facts (manufacturedDate, expirationDate, retestDate, lotStatus); the Kit sub-object captures blinded-packaging-unit facts (kitNumber, treatmentAssignment for blinded studies, assignmentDate). Dispensation tracking happens via the Visit Activity (activityType=IP_ADMINISTRATION) with STRUCTURED taskValue carrying dose/route/lot/kit references — the universal substrate covers IP administration without specialized Dispensation entity.

crosswalks.fhir: fhir:MedicationKnowledge (for drugs/biologics); fhir:DeviceDefinition (for devices); fhir:Substance (for raw substances)
crosswalks.usdm: usdm:StudyIntervention + usdm:AdministrableProduct (USDM separates the intervention from the administrable product; TOP collapses for operator UX)
crosswalks.sdtm: sdtm:EX (Exposure — captures per-dose dispensation; the IP definition is an SDTM define.xml SUPPDM or vendor extension)
crosswalks.ich: ICH E6(R3) Section 2.7 (IP Management); 21 CFR 312.6 (Labeling); ICH M11 IP fields

attributes.name: investigationalProductId
attributes.doc: Globally unique NGSI-LD identifier.

attributes.name: investigationalProductName
attributes.doc: Operator-friendly name (e.g., 'Drug X', 'BioCar-T-001', 'NeuroMonitor v2').

attributes.name: genericName
attributes.doc: Generic / non-proprietary name (when applicable).

attributes.name: brandName
attributes.doc: Brand / proprietary name (when commercially available, e.g., for active comparators or reformulations).

attributes.name: investigationalProductType
attributes.doc: Coarse type discriminator. DRUG for small-molecule pharmaceuticals; BIOLOGIC for biologics including monoclonal antibodies, vaccines, cell/gene therapies; DEVICE for medical devices; DIAGNOSTIC for in vitro diagnostics; COMBINATION_PRODUCT for drug-device or biologic-device combinations; OTHER for catch-all. Per FIRST-PRINCIPLES universal-substrate posture, this is a category discriminator for sort/filter, NOT a shape discriminator — the entity is the same shape regardless of type.

attributes.name: mechanismOfAction
attributes.doc: Brief description of how the product works (e.g., 'PD-1 checkpoint inhibitor', 'CAR-T cell therapy targeting CD19').

attributes.name: dosageForm
attributes.doc: Dosage form (TABLET / CAPSULE / SOLUTION / SUSPENSION / INJECTION / INFUSION / TOPICAL / IMPLANT / DEVICE — free text accepts NCIt or FHIR Medication.form codes).

attributes.name: routeOfAdministration
attributes.doc: Primary route of administration (ORAL / IV / IM / SC / TOPICAL / INTRATHECAL / etc.).

attributes.name: strength
attributes.doc: Strength / concentration (e.g., '200 mg', '50 mg/mL', '10^7 cells/dose').

attributes.name: isBlinded
attributes.doc: Whether this IP is dispensed under blinding (true requires Kit sub-objects with treatmentAssignment for IRT-managed kit assignment; false means open-label).

attributes.name: regulatoryStatus
attributes.doc: Regulatory status of the product. INVESTIGATIONAL for true IND/IDE products; APPROVED_OFF_LABEL when an approved drug is investigated for a new indication; EXPANDED_ACCESS for compassionate-use frameworks; MARKETED_COMPARATOR for active comparator arms using approved drugs; OTHER for catch-all.

attributes.name: regulatorySource
attributes.doc: Regulatory authority and identifier (e.g., 'FDA IND #123456', 'EMA EudraCT 2024-001234-56', 'PMDA Notification #...').

attributes.name: biomedicalConceptCode
attributes.doc: NCIt C-code for the substance / device. References COSMoS BC catalog or external substance registries (UNII, RxNorm).

attributes.name: investigationalProductStatus
attributes.doc: Lifecycle of the IP across all studies that use it. PLANNED for pre-IND; IN_DEVELOPMENT for pre-first-in-human; IN_USE for actively-administered; DISCONTINUED for development paused; WITHDRAWN for safety-driven removal. NGSI-LD temporal property.

attributes.name: validFrom
attributes.doc: NGSI-LD temporal property: when this IP record became operationally valid.

relationships.name: suppliedBySponsor
relationships.doc: The Sponsor that supplies / manufactures / imports this IP. Per 21 CFR 312.6 the IP supplier is regulatorily accountable for product labeling, lot tracking, and dispensation oversight.

relationships.name: forStudy
relationships.doc: The Studies that use this IP. 0..N because some IPs are used across multiple studies (platform trials, basket trials, expanded-access programs).

relationships.name: hasLot
relationships.doc: Manufacturing lots / batches of this IP. Each Lot has its own manufacturing date, expiration, release status, and accountability chain.

relationships.name: hasKit
relationships.doc: Blinded packaging units (kits) prepared from this IP. Required when isBlinded=true (SHACL invariant). Each Kit ties to a Lot and is assigned to a Participant via IRT.

relationships.name: governedByDocument
relationships.doc: Documents governing this IP — Investigator's Brochure, IP handling instructions, product label, Certificate of Analysis (lot-specific), IP destruction record. Per ICH E6(R3) Section 2.7.

ctas.note: Create a new IP record with type, regulatory source (IND #), mechanism of action, blinding status.
Create a Lot sub-object with lotNumber, manufacturedDate, expirationDate, releaseDate. Required for any actual dispensation.
Create Kit sub-objects from a Lot for blinded studies; each Kit has kitNumber + treatmentAssignment (the IRT randomization code).
Transition investigationalProductStatus across the development lifecycle (PLANNED → IN_DEVELOPMENT → IN_USE → DISCONTINUED / WITHDRAWN).
When a manufacturing or safety issue surfaces, transition Lot.lotStatus to RECALLED; cascades to Kit reconciliation workflow at Sites.

sub_objects.role: a manufacturing batch of the InvestigationalProduct
sub_objects.description: A Lot represents a single manufacturing batch of an InvestigationalProduct. Per ICH E6(R3) Section 2.7, IP must be tracked at lot level for accountability, expiry management, and recall response. The Lot carries manufacturing date, release date, expiration date, retest date (when applicable), manufacturer / facility identifiers, and lot status. Dispensation events at Visits reference the Lot used (via Visit's STRUCTURED taskValue or via Kit assignment for blinded studies).

crosswalks.ich: ICH E6(R3) Section 2.7; 21 CFR 312.62 (Investigator records — drug disposition)

attributes.name: lotNumber
attributes.doc: Sponsor-assigned lot identifier (e.g., 'DRX-2026-0117'). Operator-grounded — what gets printed on the IP label and tracked in pharmacy.

attributes.name: manufacturedDate
attributes.doc: Date the lot was manufactured.

attributes.name: releaseDate
attributes.doc: Date the lot was released for clinical use (post-QC / post-Certificate-of-Analysis).

attributes.name: expirationDate
attributes.doc: Expiration date. Hard SHACL: lots cannot be administered after expiration; lotStatus=EXPIRED requires this date <= today.

attributes.name: retestDate
attributes.doc: Date by which the lot must be re-tested (for stability-extension lots). Optional — only applies when stability data supports retest extension.

attributes.name: manufacturer
attributes.doc: Name of the manufacturer (when distinct from suppliedBySponsor — e.g., contract manufacturer).

attributes.name: manufacturingFacility
attributes.doc: Manufacturing facility identifier or address.

attributes.name: quantityProduced
attributes.doc: Number of units produced in this lot (kits, vials, tablets — unit depends on dosageForm).

attributes.name: lotStatus
attributes.doc: Lot lifecycle. NGSI-LD temporal property. MANUFACTURING during production; RELEASED after QC sign-off; IN_DISTRIBUTION during shipment; DISTRIBUTED when arrived at sites; EXPIRED past expiration date; RECALLED for safety / quality recall; DESTROYED post-reconciliation.

relationships.name: governedByDocument
relationships.doc: Documents governing this lot — Certificate of Analysis (lot-specific), lot release record, lot destruction record.

sub_objects.role: a blinded packaging unit assigned to a Participant
sub_objects.description: A Kit is a single blinded packaging unit prepared from a Lot, assigned to a Participant via IRT (Interactive Response Technology). Required for blinded studies where treatment assignment is masked at the dispensation level. Each Kit carries the kit number (printed on the label), its source Lot, the treatment assignment code (the IRT-issued randomization code; the unblinding key is held separately by the IRT vendor), and lifecycle status (PACKAGED → IN_INVENTORY → DISPENSED → RETURNED → RECONCILED).

crosswalks.sdtm: sdtm:EX (kit number tracked per dispensation row)
crosswalks.ich: ICH E6(R3) Section 2.7; ICH E9 Section 2.3 (Randomization and Blinding)

attributes.name: kitNumber
attributes.doc: Sponsor / IRT-assigned kit identifier (e.g., 'KIT-00042'). Operator-grounded — what gets scanned at dispensation.

attributes.name: packagedDate
attributes.doc: Date the kit was packaged from its Lot.

attributes.name: treatmentAssignment
attributes.doc: IRT-issued treatment-assignment code. For blinded studies, this is a coded label (e.g., 'A', 'B', 'PLACEBO_GROUP_2') whose unblinding key is held separately by the IRT vendor; the substrate carries the code without the unblinding map.

attributes.name: assignmentDate
attributes.doc: Date the kit was assigned to a Participant (typically the randomization or dispensation date).

attributes.name: kitStatus
attributes.doc: Kit lifecycle. NGSI-LD temporal property. PACKAGED at packaging facility; IN_INVENTORY at site pharmacy; ASSIGNED when IRT issues to Participant (pre-dispensation); DISPENSED when handed to Participant; RETURNED when unused returned to pharmacy; RECONCILED at end-of-trial reconciliation; DESTROYED per IP destruction record.

relationships.name: belongsToLot
relationships.doc: The Lot this Kit was packaged from. Each Kit traces back to exactly one Lot for accountability.

relationships.name: assignedToParticipant
relationships.doc: The Participant the Kit is assigned to (post-IRT-assignment). 0..1 because Kits in IN_INVENTORY haven't been assigned yet. SHACL: kitStatus=ASSIGNED or DISPENSED requires this to be populated.

horizontals.description: The legal/business entity. Sponsor and Site (TOP top-levels) reference Organization for their corporate identity and roll-up structure. Modeled after USDM v3 Organization, with the addition of parentOrganization for explicit corporate hierarchy. Cross-industry: Organizations also represent operating companies in energy (refiners, EPCs), manufacturing (parent corp, plants, contract manufacturers), and other domains.
horizontals.ooux_editor_note: Introduced in v0.2 to resolve the multi-business-unit problem (J&J vs Janssen R&D vs Innovative Medicine) and the site-network problem (Elevate Research vs its locations). USDM keeps Sponsor as a Code type on Organization; TOP keeps Sponsor as a top-level for operator UX while backing it with Organization for corporate truth.

attributes.name: organizationId
attributes.doc: Globally unique NGSI-LD identifier.

attributes.name: organizationName
attributes.doc: Display name.

attributes.name: legalName
attributes.doc: Registered legal name.

attributes.name: organizationType
attributes.doc: Role classification (multi-valued in practice via separate role assignments). Aligns with USDM Organization.type Code.

attributes.name: identifierScheme
attributes.doc: Scheme for the identifier value. USDM-aligned.

attributes.name: identifier
attributes.doc: Identifier value scoped by identifierScheme.

attributes.name: legalAddress
attributes.doc: Headquarters/registered address (line1, line2, city, region, postalCode).

attributes.name: country
attributes.doc: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of registration.

attributes.name: website
attributes.doc: Organization website.

attributes.name: status
attributes.doc: Lifecycle state.

attributes.name: employeeSizeBand
attributes.doc: Banded employee count for the Organization. Corporate-scope fact; does not vary per Study, so lives here rather than on the per-Study Sponsor entity. Bands rather than exact counts because exact headcount is volatile and rarely needed for Sponsor portfolio analysis.

attributes.name: fiscalYearEnd
attributes.doc: Fiscal year end as MM-DD (e.g., '12-31' for calendar year, '03-31' for many UK/Asian companies, '09-30' for US federal). Corporate-scope fact; useful for budget alignment, contract renewal cycles, and forecasting.

attributes.name: primaryTherapeuticAreas
attributes.doc: Comma-separated or JSON array of the Organization's portfolio therapeutic areas (oncology, cardiology, infectious disease, rare disease, etc.). Corporate-scope: Pfizer's TA spread is many; J&J Innovative Medicine's TA spread is a subset. The per-Study TA focus lives on Sponsor.primaryTherapeuticArea. Multi-value semantics: native NGSI-LD multi-value once the broker layer is in place; for the strawman emission, comma-separated string is the lowest-risk encoding.

relationships.name: parentOrganization
relationships.doc: Self-reference for corporate hierarchy. J&J Innovative Medicine.parentOrganization = J&J. Empty for root-level entities.

relationships.name: hasChildOrganization
relationships.doc: Inverse of parentOrganization. Convenience relationship for downward traversal.

relationships.name: managesSite
relationships.doc: Sites operationally managed by this Organization. USDM Organization.managedSites.

relationships.name: playsSponsorRole
relationships.doc: Sponsor entities backed by this Organization in specific Studies.

relationships.name: isLocatedIn
relationships.doc: Country of registration; flagged-missing object in OOUX v0.2.
relationships._targetMissing: Country is a flagged-missing object in OOUX v0.2 (#57).

horizontals.role: TMF / eTMF / ISF document or essential record
horizontals.description: Document represents any file or formal record associated with the trial. ICH E6(R3) Appendix C is the canonical record taxonomy: records are categorized by purpose (what they contribute to under GCP) and by responsible party. The TMF Reference Model and the ISF section taxonomy are the legacy operational views that map onto R3 purposes. TOP carries both axes: essentialRecordPurpose (R3 primary) and isfSection (legacy operational view).
horizontals.ooux_editor_note: v0.2.0 expansion (2026-05-08): added essentialRecordPurpose (R3 Appendix C purpose taxonomy — currently placeholder enum, full enum requires Appendix C transcription pending PDF access), responsibleParty (R3 record-owner discriminator), isfSection (legacy operational view), and richer documentType enum aligned to ISF section identifiers. R3 anchors are canonical; ISF section taxonomy is a secondary mapping for operational continuity.

attributes.name: documentType
attributes.doc: Document subtype taxonomy aligned to ISF section identifiers and TMF Reference Model zones. Operational/UX label.

attributes.name: documentStatus
attributes.doc: Lifecycle state.

attributes.name: essentialRecordPurpose
attributes._partialEnum: ICH E6(R3) Appendix C purpose taxonomy. Full enum requires Appendix C PDF transcription; placeholder values cover the seven purposes named in R3 extracts.
attributes.doc: R3 Appendix C purpose: what this record contributes to under GCP.

attributes.name: responsibleParty
attributes.doc: R3 Appendix C: party responsible for creating and maintaining this record.

attributes.name: isfSection
attributes.doc: Legacy operational view: ISF section identifier (e.g., '11.1' for Signature Sheet, '14.3' for IP Accountability Log, '22.1' for Sponsor correspondences). Free-text rather than enum because ISF flavors vary (Singapore HSA, FDA, EMA all have variations).

attributes.name: etmfLocation
attributes.doc: URI of the document in its eTMF/ISF system.

attributes.name: retentionUntil
attributes.doc: Retention end date per regulatory/contractual requirements.

attributes.name: language
attributes.doc: ISO 639-1 code.

horizontals.role: per-Study Site participation pivot — the operational join entity for Site x Study
horizontals.description: StudySite captures a Site's role on a specific Study. Every per-Site-per-Study fact (siteNumber, enrollmentTarget, IRB approval status, oversight tier, activation/deactivation dates, per-Study staff delegation, per-Study Systems, per-Study contracts) lives here. StudySite is the entry point for Participants enrolled at this Site for this Study and Visit-occurrences happening at this Site for this Study. The operational hierarchy runs Site -> StudySite -> Study -> Protocol -> SOA -> Visit -> Activity. StudySite is reached by the operator clicking 'this Site on this Study' and is where most of the day-to-day workflow lives.
horizontals.ooux_editor_note: v0.2.0 introduction (2026-05-08): introduced as a new horizontal under TOP commons to resolve the Site-operational-vs-per-Study-participation tension. Crosswalks to usdm:StudySite (which is itself thin in USDM v3 — id+name+description+country only). TOP carries substantially more on StudySite because the operational pivot needs the relationships USDM doesn't model. See site-planning.md for the architectural discussion. The OOUX Visit->Site and Participant->Site direct edges are corrections that surface when those top-levels lift; both should point at StudySite, not Site directly.

attributes.name: studySiteId
attributes.doc: Globally unique NGSI-LD identifier for this Site-Study pair.

attributes.name: siteNumber
attributes.doc: Site number assigned by the sponsor for this Study. Distinct from Site.siteId (which is global).

attributes.name: studySiteStatus
attributes.doc: Per-Study lifecycle state. R3 Section 2.6 grounds the termination/suspension framing.

attributes.name: activationDate
attributes.doc: Date the Site was activated for this Study (Site Initiation Visit completed; first patient can be enrolled).

attributes.name: deactivationDate
attributes.doc: Date the Site was deactivated for this Study (close-out completed). Empty for currently-active StudySite records.

attributes.name: enrollmentTarget
attributes.doc: Target number of Participants for this Site for this Study.

attributes.name: actualEnrollment
attributes.doc: Current count of Participants enrolled at this Site for this Study. Could be a derived attribute via SPARQL aggregation; surfaced as a stored attribute for operational view speed.

attributes.name: oversightTier
attributes.doc: R3 Section 3.10 risk-based monitoring tier for this Site on this Study. Drives Sponsor monitoring frequency and Audit prioritization. The same Site can be HIGH on a complex oncology trial and LOW on a simple registry — hence per-StudySite, not per-Site.

attributes.name: recruitmentPotential
attributes.doc: Per-protocol estimate of recruitable Participants. Distinct from Site.totalTrialsConducted (lifetime) and Site.averageEnrollmentRate (cross-trial). R3 Section 2.2 grounds the requirement.

attributes.name: staffingAdequate
attributes.doc: Per-protocol staffing adequacy. R3 Section 2.2 — adequacy depends on protocol complexity, so per-StudySite.

attributes.name: essentialRecordsRetentionUntil
attributes.doc: Date until which essential records for this StudySite must be retained. Per-Study because retention requirements vary by jurisdiction x Study type.

attributes.name: irbApprovalStatus
attributes.doc: Current IRB approval status for this StudySite.

attributes.name: regulatoryAuthorizationStatus
attributes.doc: Per-Study regulatory authorization status (FDA, EMA, etc., per jurisdiction).

attributes.name: validFrom
attributes.doc: Datetime from which this StudySite participation is operationally valid. Mirrors Sponsor's NGSI-LD-native temporal property for relationship handoffs (e.g., when a Site changes Principal Investigator mid-study).

relationships.name: forSite
relationships.doc: The Site this StudySite participation belongs to.

relationships.name: forStudy
relationships.doc: The Study this Site is participating in.

relationships.name: hasPrincipalInvestigator
relationships.doc: The per-Study Principal Investigator at this Site. R3 Section 2.1 GCP-required. The Person must be drawn from forSite.hasStaff (SHACL invariant). Temporal handoffs use validFrom/validUntil on the relationship.

relationships.name: delegatesAuthorityTo
relationships.doc: Per-Study Delegation of Authority Log roster. R3 Section 2.3.3 GCP-required. Each delegation carries role (sub-investigator, study coordinator, regulatory coordinator, pharmacist, etc.), scope, validFrom, validUntil. Each Person must be drawn from forSite.hasStaff.

relationships.name: hasIRB
relationships.doc: Per-Study IRB(s)/IEC(s) of record for this StudySite. 1..N for multi-IRB cases.

relationships.name: submitsTo
relationships.doc: Per-jurisdiction regulatory authorities this StudySite files with. R3 Section 2.3.5 inspection right anchored here.
relationships._targetMissing: RegulatoryAuthority is a flagged-missing horizontal in OOUX v0.2.

relationships.name: hasContract
relationships.doc: Site-level contracts for this StudySite participation: CTA, CDA, MTA, DTA, etc.
relationships._targetMissing: Contract is a flagged-missing horizontal in OOUX v0.2.

relationships.name: hasStudyStartupPackage
relationships.doc: The Study Startup Package distributed to this Site for this Study.
relationships._targetMissing: StudyStartupPackage is in OOUX v0.2 catalog but not yet in TOP source.

relationships.name: hostsVisit
relationships.doc: Visit-occurrences at this Site for this Study. Inverse of Visit.atStudySite (when Visit lifts).
relationships._targetMissing: Visit is a top-level in OOUX (one of the locked 8) but not yet in TOP source.

relationships.name: hasParticipant
relationships.doc: Participants enrolled at this Site for this Study. The OOUX Participant->Site direct edge is a correction: should be Participant->StudySite when Participant lifts.
relationships._targetMissing: Participant is a top-level in OOUX (one of the locked 8) but not yet in TOP source.

relationships.name: hasMonitoringVisit
relationships.doc: Per-Study monitoring visits at this Site.
relationships._targetMissing: MonitoringVisit is in OOUX v0.2 catalog but not yet in TOP source.

relationships.name: hasAudit
relationships.doc: Per-Study audits at this Site.
relationships._targetMissing: Audit is in OOUX v0.2 catalog but not yet in TOP source.

relationships.name: engagesVendor
relationships.doc: Per-Study Site-engaged vendors (R3 Section 2.3.4 documented agreements). Sponsor-engaged vendors are different — those are the Sponsor's; this is the Site's per-Study direct engagement (after R3 Section 2.3 Investigator's right of acceptance/refusal).

relationships.name: usesLaboratory
relationships.doc: Per-Study Site-engaged laboratories (Organization with organizationType=LABORATORY). Distinct from Site.usesSystem for the Site's own internal lab system.

relationships.name: usesSystem
relationships.doc: Per-Study Systems used at this Site participation: Sponsor-provisioned EDC, per-Study IRT, per-Study eTMF, etc. The System's three-axis pattern (operatedBy / usedBy / oversightHeldBy) on the System entity captures the GCP responsibility allocation.

relationships.name: reportsTo
relationships.doc: Per-Study reporting target (R3 Sections 2.7, 2.13: safety, data, noncompliance reporting from Site to Sponsor).

relationships.name: hasEssentialRecord
relationships.doc: Per-Study essential records (with Document.essentialRecordPurpose set per R3 Appendix C and Document.responsibleParty=INVESTIGATOR_INSTITUTION). Distinct from Site.hasInstitutionalDocument (study-independent institutional records).

relationships.name: hasEnrollment
relationships.doc: Enrollment records at this StudySite.
relationships._targetMissing: Enrollment is in OOUX v0.2 catalog but not yet in TOP source.

horizontals.description: Person represents any individual involved in trial conduct: principal investigators, sub-investigators, study coordinators, regulatory coordinators, pharmacists, study nurses, lab techs, monitors, sponsor staff, regulators, IRB members. Persons exist independently of any specific Site or Study; their roles in specific contexts are captured via relationship targets and PersonRole assignments. USDM models AssignedPerson as nested inside StudyRole — TOP makes Person a horizontal so it can be referenced consistently across the operational chain (Site.hasStaff, StudySite.hasPrincipalInvestigator, StudySite.delegatesAuthorityTo, Sponsor.employs, OversightBody.members, Audit.auditors).
horizontals.ooux_editor_note: v0.2.0 introduction (2026-05-08): lifted from OOUX Person object. Required by both Site and StudySite person relationships; could not be flagged-missing for the Site spec (R3 Section 2.3.3 GCP-required Delegation of Authority Log needs real Person targets). USDM crosswalk: usdm:AssignedPerson (the operational/participation lens) plus usdm:StudyRole (the role-typing lens). PersonRole is currently embedded as a per-relationship attribute (role enum on delegatesAuthorityTo); a separate PersonRole sub-object lifts in v0.3 if the role taxonomy grows beyond what an enum can carry.

attributes.name: personId
attributes.doc: Globally unique NGSI-LD identifier.

attributes.name: title
attributes.doc: Professional title (Dr., Prof., MD, RN, PharmD, etc.).

attributes.name: jobTitle
attributes.doc: USDM AssignedPerson.jobTitle. The functional role at the employing Organization (e.g., Director of Clinical Research, Senior CRA, Study Coordinator).

attributes.name: orcid
attributes.doc: ORCID identifier.

attributes.name: npi
attributes.doc: US National Provider Identifier (where applicable).

attributes.name: preferredLanguages
attributes.doc: Comma-separated ISO 639-1 codes.

attributes.name: gcpTrainingStatus
attributes.doc: GCP training currency. Per-Person fact independent of any Site or Study.

attributes.name: status
attributes.doc: Lifecycle state.

relationships.name: employedBy
relationships.doc: Primary employing Organization. USDM AssignedPerson.organizationId. A Person can have multiple StudySite/Sponsor relationships independent of where they're employed.

relationships.name: hasCredential
relationships.doc: Credentials this Person holds (medical license, specialty board certifications, GCP certificate, etc.).

relationships.name: hasTrainingRecord
relationships.doc: Training records for this Person (study-specific GCP, study-specific protocol training, etc.).
relationships._targetMissing: TrainingRecord is in OOUX v0.2 (#77) but not yet in TOP source.

horizontals.role: computerised system used in clinical trial conduct
horizontals.description: System represents a computerised system used in trial conduct: EDC, CTMS, eTMF/ISF, IRT/RTSM, safety database, EMR, ELN, eCOA/ePRO platform, eConsent platform, sample management system, etc. Systems carry their R3 Section 4.3 governance attributes (validation, security, access controls, release status, contingency plans, support) and a three-axis role pattern (operatedBy / usedBy / oversightHeldBy) that captures GCP responsibility allocation.
horizontals.ooux_editor_note: v0.2.0 introduction (2026-05-08): lifted from OOUX System object. R3-aligned attribute set per Section 4.3 (procedures, training, security, validation, release, contingencies, support, user/access management). Three-axis role pattern resolves the System ownership/use/visibility question: visibility follows from oversight under R3 Section 3.9 (Sponsor) and Section 2.12 (Site); the schema captures who is GCP-responsible, and presentation follows. The System horizontal earns a dedicated v0.3 spec eventually; in v0.2 it serves Site/StudySite/Sponsor.

crosswalks.fhir: fhir:Device (where the System is a medical-device-class platform; otherwise no direct FHIR equivalent)

attributes.name: vendor
attributes.doc: Vendor name (e.g., 'Veeva', 'Medidata', 'Oracle', 'Castor').

attributes.name: productName
attributes.doc: Product name (e.g., 'Vault eTMF', 'Rave EDC', 'Siebel CTMS', 'CDMS').

attributes.name: instanceId
attributes.doc: Tenant or instance identifier (e.g., 'mskcc-prod', 'pfizer-tenant-7').

attributes.name: baseUrl
attributes.doc: Base URL for the System instance. May be unpopulated when the consumer of the System record does not have access to the instance details (R3 Section 3.9 oversight-as-relationship framing).

attributes.name: systemType
attributes.doc: Functional system type.

attributes.name: validatedFor21CfrPart11
attributes.doc: FDA 21 CFR Part 11 validation status.

attributes.name: validatedForEuAnnex11
attributes.doc: EU EudraLex Annex 11 validation status.

attributes.name: validationStatus
attributes.doc: R3 Section 4.3.4 validation lifecycle state.

attributes.name: validationApproach
attributes.doc: R3 Section 4.3.4 risk-based validation approach.

attributes.name: releaseStatus
attributes.doc: R3 Section 4.3.5 release lifecycle.

attributes.name: auditTrailEnabled
attributes.doc: R3 Section 4.2.2 audit-trail capability.

relationships.name: operatedBy
relationships.doc: The Organization that operates this System and is responsible for its R3 Section 4.3 obligations (validation, security, access controls, release, contingency, support). A Sponsor's EDC has operatedBy -> Pfizer (or Pfizer's CRO under documented agreement); a Site's local source-document repository has operatedBy -> MSKCC.

relationships.name: usedBy
relationships.doc: Organizations that use this System for trial activities. A Sponsor-operated EDC is used by both Sponsor staff and authorized Site staff; the same System has multiple usedBy triples.

relationships.name: oversightHeldBy
relationships.doc: The Organization holding GCP-mandated oversight responsibility for this System's use in trial conduct. R3 Section 3.9 (Sponsor for trial-critical Sponsor-delegated Systems) or Section 2.12 (Site for Site-internal records). For sponsor-critical Systems (EDC, CTMS, IRT, SAFETY_DB), oversightHeldBy must be a Sponsor (SHACL invariant); the IIT case where Site=Sponsor exempts via the Site.belongsToOrganization == Sponsor.belongsToOrganization equality test.

relationships.name: proceduresDocumentRef
relationships.doc: R3 Section 4.3.1 procedures document.

relationships.name: securityAssessmentRef
relationships.doc: R3 Section 4.3.3 security assessment.

relationships.name: accessControlPolicyRef
relationships.doc: R3 Section 4.3.8 access control policy.

relationships.name: contingencyPlanRef
relationships.doc: R3 Section 4.3.6 contingency / disaster-recovery plan.

relationships.name: supportContact
relationships.doc: R3 Section 4.3.7 technical support point of contact.

horizontals.role: operational record of events, actions, or observations
horizontals.description: Log represents records of events, actions, or observations that occur during trial conduct. Logs are append-only by GCP discipline (R3 Section 4.2.2) — entries cannot be retroactively edited. Logs are distinct from Documents in that they have lifecycle behavior (entry, review, sign-off) and are inherently temporal sequences. Covers Subject Logs (ISF section 13), IP Accountability Logs (14.3), IP Storage Temperature Logs (14.5), Site Visit Logs (16.1), Sample Handling Logs (18.1), SAE Tracking Logs (19.1), Communication Logs (22), and others.
horizontals.ooux_editor_note: v0.2.0 introduction (2026-05-08): lifted from OOUX Log object. Required by Site spec because ISF sections 13/14.3/14.5/16.1/18.1/19.1/22 are all log-shaped. CommunicationLog (ISF section 22 correspondences) folds in as logType=COMMUNICATION rather than as a separate horizontal — saves an entity, exercises the polysemous-verb-split discipline by keeping logType discriminator clean.

crosswalks.fhir: fhir:AuditEvent (for system-generated audit-trail logs); fhir:DocumentReference (for archival-form logs)

attributes.name: logType
attributes.doc: The log's discriminator. CommunicationLog folds in as COMMUNICATION; AuditTrail folds in as AUDIT_TRAIL.

attributes.name: logTitle
attributes.doc: Human-readable title of the log (e.g., 'IP Storage Temperature — Freezer F-12 — 2026 Q1').

attributes.name: closedDate
attributes.doc: Empty for currently-active logs.

attributes.name: status
attributes.doc: Lifecycle state.

attributes.name: entryCount
attributes.doc: Count of entries in this log; aggregate signal.

attributes.name: retentionUntil
attributes.doc: Date until which this log must be retained per regulatory/contractual requirements.

relationships.name: forStudySite
relationships.doc: The StudySite this log belongs to. Most operational logs are per-StudySite. Empty for Site-wide logs (e.g., institutional audit trail) which use forSite instead.

relationships.name: forSite
relationships.doc: The Site this log belongs to, when the log is study-independent (e.g., institutional GCP training log).

relationships.name: forStudy
relationships.doc: The Study this log belongs to, when the log is Sponsor-side and not Site-scoped.

relationships.name: forSystem
relationships.doc: The System this log records (e.g., audit-trail logs are System-scoped under R3 Section 4.2.2).

relationships.name: owner
relationships.doc: The Person responsible for maintaining this log.

relationships.name: correspondent
relationships.doc: For logType=COMMUNICATION: the parties this correspondence log captures exchanges with (Sponsor, IRB, RegulatoryAuthority, central lab, vendor). 0..N because some correspondence logs aggregate across multiple parties.

relationships.name: forEquipment
relationships.doc: The Equipment this log is about (e.g., a temperature log for freezer F-12). Distinct from forSystem which captures the monitoring System; logs about Equipment with IoT monitoring will populate both forEquipment and forSystem.

horizontals.role: physical device, instrument, or apparatus used in trial conduct
horizontals.description: Equipment represents physical devices used in clinical-trial conduct: refrigerators, freezers, centrifuges, scales, ECG machines, ultrasound, blood pressure devices, glucometers, pulse oximeters, etc. Equipment is distinct from System (computerised systems): a freezer with IoT temperature monitoring is Equipment + a System (the monitoring platform), linked via Equipment.monitoredBySystem. Under R3, Equipment carries qualification, calibration, maintenance, and training discipline; computerised-systems aspects (validation, audit trails, access controls per R3 Section 4.3) live on the linked System.
horizontals.ooux_editor_note: v0.2.0 introduction (2026-05-08): lifted from OOUX Equipment object. equipmentBinding enum locked from OOUX hierarchy boundary decision (SITE_BOUND, VENDOR_OWNED, PARTICIPANT_LOANED). Added equipmentClass enum to discriminate freezer vs centrifuge vs ECG vs participant-loaned wearable, etc. Added monitoredBySystem to capture the Equipment+System interaction for IoT-monitored Equipment (the freezer-with-cloud-temp-monitor pattern, ISF section 14.5). Equipment is the physical asset (calibration, maintenance, qualification); System is the computerised layer (R3 Section 4.3 obligations); Log captures the temporal trail (R3 Section 4.2.2 audit trail).

crosswalks.omop: omop:DEVICE_EXPOSURE (per-use event at Visit-occurrence; Equipment entity itself has no direct OMOP table)
crosswalks.obo: obi:device (partial; OBI covers some equipment classes but coverage is fragmentary)

attributes.name: equipmentName
attributes.doc: Display name (e.g., 'Freezer F-12 Pharmacy', 'Eppendorf 5810R Centrifuge').

attributes.name: equipmentClass
attributes.doc: Equipment class taxonomy. Covers most clinical-trial Equipment classes including participant-loaned wearables.

attributes.name: equipmentBinding
attributes.doc: OOUX-locked binding pattern: SITE_BOUND for institution-owned equipment at a Site; VENDOR_OWNED for sponsor- or vendor-supplied equipment used at a Site; PARTICIPANT_LOANED for equipment loaned to a participant (wearables, BP cuff, glucometer).

attributes.name: status
attributes.doc: Lifecycle state.

attributes.name: locationDescription
attributes.doc: Free-text location (e.g., 'Pharmacy Room 3, second shelf'). For structured location, use atStorageLocation relationship via the StorageLocation horizontal.

attributes.name: temperatureRange
attributes.doc: For temperature-controlled Equipment: {minTempC, maxTempC, units}. Distinct from monitored temperature readings, which live in Log entries.

relationships.name: atSite
relationships.doc: The Site where this Equipment is located. Inverse of Site.hostsEquipment. 0..1 because VENDOR_OWNED equipment may move between sites; PARTICIPANT_LOANED equipment is at a Participant.

relationships.name: atStudySite
relationships.doc: The StudySites this Equipment is dedicated to. Empty for Site-shared Equipment (a general-purpose centrifuge); populated for per-Study dedicated Equipment (a study-specific freezer).

relationships.name: vendor
relationships.doc: The Organization that manufactured or supplies this Equipment. Organization with organizationType=VENDOR.

relationships.name: monitoredBySystem
relationships.doc: Computerised System(s) that monitor or control this Equipment. A pharmacy-grade freezer with cloud-based temperature logging has monitoredBySystem pointing to the IoT temperature monitor System. The Equipment-System split keeps R3 Section 4.3 obligations on the System and Equipment-qualification obligations on the Equipment.

relationships.name: responsiblePerson
relationships.doc: The Person responsible for maintaining/operating this Equipment.

relationships.name: hasCredential
relationships.doc: Equipment-level credentials: calibration certificates, validation certificates, vendor certifications.

relationships.name: hasUserManual
relationships.doc: User manual document.

horizontals.role: addressable storage point at a Site
horizontals.description: StorageLocation represents an addressable storage point: a slot in a freezer rack, a shelf in a refrigerator, a position in a sample matrix, a drawer in a secure cabinet, an offsite vault location. StorageLocation is distinct from Equipment: an Equipment freezer provides multiple StorageLocations (its slots/shelves/racks); a secure cabinet (Equipment) provides drawer StorageLocations. Some StorageLocations are not inside Equipment (an ambient-temperature shelf in a controlled-access pharmacy room).
horizontals.ooux_editor_note: v0.2.0 introduction (2026-05-08): lifted from OOUX-flagged sub-object 'Storage Location (site-bound)' under the Site OOUX entry. Modeled as a horizontal because it's also referenced from Sample and InvestigationalProduct holdings; sub-object-under-Site would be too narrow. The Equipment/StorageLocation split lets a freezer (Equipment) provide many slots (StorageLocations) without conflating the two.

attributes.name: locationName
attributes.doc: Display name (e.g., 'Freezer F-12, Rack 3, Slot A4').

attributes.name: temperatureRangeC
attributes.doc: Standard temperature ranges in Celsius. For non-standard ranges document via the linked Equipment.temperatureRange.

attributes.name: capacity
attributes.doc: Total capacity (count or volume).

attributes.name: capacityUnit
attributes.doc: Unit of capacity (slots, mL, etc.).

attributes.name: currentOccupancy
attributes.doc: Current count of items stored.

attributes.name: gpsCoordinates
attributes.doc: Lat/long for offsite vaults (e.g., '40.7128,-74.0060').

attributes.name: status
attributes.doc: Lifecycle state.

relationships.name: atSite
relationships.doc: The Site this StorageLocation belongs to. Inverse of Site.hasStorageLocation.

relationships.name: inEquipment
relationships.doc: The Equipment this StorageLocation is housed in (when applicable). A slot in freezer F-12 has inEquipment pointing to Freezer F-12; an ambient pharmacy shelf has no inEquipment.

relationships.name: monitoredBySystem
relationships.doc: Computerised systems that monitor this StorageLocation (inventory tracking, slot-level temperature monitoring).

horizontals.role: qualification, certification, or accreditation held by a Person, Site, or Equipment
horizontals.description: Credential represents a qualification, certification, license, or accreditation. Credentials apply polymorphically to Persons (medical licenses, GCP certificates, board certifications), Sites (CLIA certification, JCAHO accreditation, ISO 9001), and Equipment (calibration certificates, validation certificates). Each Credential carries an issuing authority, an issue/expiry window, and a verification status, plus an optional reference to the certificate Document.
horizontals.ooux_editor_note: v0.2.0 introduction (2026-05-08): lifted from OOUX Credential object. Polymorphic targets (Person, Site, Equipment) modeled as separate optional relationships rather than a single polymorphic 'forSubject'; matches OOUX (Credential has 0-1 Person + 0-1 Site) and adds Equipment for equipment-level credentials (calibration certificates). SHACL invariant enforces exactly-one-target.

attributes.name: credentialNumber
attributes.doc: Identifier issued by the issuing authority.

attributes.name: issuingAuthorityName
attributes.doc: Free-text name when the issuing authority is not modeled as an Organization (e.g., 'Texas Medical Board'); use issuingAuthorityOrg relationship for modeled authorities.

attributes.name: status
attributes.doc: Lifecycle state.

relationships.name: forPerson
relationships.doc: Person this Credential is held by (medical license, GCP certificate, etc.). Mutually exclusive with forSite and forEquipment in operational practice; SHACL invariant enforces exactly-one-target.

relationships.name: forSite
relationships.doc: Site this Credential is held by (CLIA, JCAHO, etc.).

relationships.name: forEquipment
relationships.doc: Equipment this Credential is held by (calibration certificate, validation certificate).

relationships.name: issuingAuthorityOrg
relationships.doc: The Organization issuing this Credential, when modeled (e.g., American Board of Internal Medicine). Use when the issuing authority is also referenced elsewhere; otherwise use issuingAuthorityName free-text.

relationships.name: verifiedBy
relationships.doc: Person who verified this Credential.

relationships.name: referenceDocument
relationships.doc: The certificate / license / attestation document file.
